mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during expansionary periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real yields and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20d returns in > 60% of observed cases, breaking the real-rate compression logic.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent confirms robust economic expansion and corporate revenue acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 3.0 percent threshold, indicating earnings decoupling.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening household income and consumption capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases show no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day SPX direction across multiple market cycles, with p-values exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales expansion outpaces industrial production growth, supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation erode corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 48% in walk-forward testing, indicating margin resilience.
